summary:
Claim: The odontoblastic layer contains cells varying in size and shape across different regions of the tooth.
Evidence:
- Cells are typically taller and more columnar at the crown due to higher stress.
- Closer to the apex, cells are shorter for better support against compression.
Answer: D
To summarize, the odontoblastic layer exhibits morphological variations along its length, adapting to regional mechanical demands. This variation supports both structural integrity and functional needs throughout the tooth. Thus, acknowledging this range of cell sizes and shapes aligns with option D indicating "All of the above."
